Up to 11 hours[3]
Video Playback Battery Life

Up to 19 hours[10]
Battery Recharge Time

Supports battery fast charge: approximately 50% in 30 minutes[5]
External I/O Ports

2 USB Type-C® 10Gbps signaling rate (USB Power Delivery, DisplayPort™ 1.4a, HP Sleep and Charge); 1 USB Type-A 10Gbps signaling rate; 1 USB Type-A 5Gbps signaling rate; 1 HDMI-out 2.1; 1 headphone/microphone combo[19]
Energy efficiency

EPEAT® Gold with Climate+
Webcam

HP True Vision 1080p FHD IR camera with temporal noise reduction and integrated dual array digital microphones[30]
Audio Features

DTS:X® Ultra; Dual speakers; HP Audio Boost[28]
Color

Natural silver aluminum cover and keyboard frame, natural silver base
Pointing device

HP Imagepad; Precision Touchpad support
Keyboard

Full-size, backlit, soft gray keyboard with numeric keypad
Dimensions (W X D X H)

14.08 x 10.03 x 0.7 in (front); 14.08 x 10.03 x 0.73 in (rear)
Weight

3.92 lb
Warranty

1-year limited hardware warranty. Additional information available at www.support.hp.com; 1-year limited technical support for software and initial setup (from date of purchase)
Software included

McAfee Online Protection 30-day trial
Intel® Unison™; Copilot in Windows with Copilot key
1 month trial for new Microsoft 365 customers[2,25][24]
Security management

Mic mute key; Camera privacy shutter; Trusted Platform Module (Firmware TPM) support
Neural Processing Unit (NPU)

Intel® AI Boost (11 NPU TOPS)

Nancy Pelosi's challenger wants to pull an AOC. But she is no Joe Crowley

 As she watched Saikat (pronounced “Shoy-cott”) Chakrabarti, a 39-year-old former tech executive, launch his seemingly impossible challenge to Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi in San Francisco’s Mission District, Najwa Alsheikh was inspired by something she saw in him. 

“He’s like the West Coast version of (New York mayoral candidate Zohran) Mamdani,” said Alsheikh, a 43-year-old Castro district attorney and Pelosi voter. “And I think that he’s going to take this city by storm, and he has a lot of grassroots support behind him. Pelosi may have been right for the time and place that she was in, but her politics have not grown at all. They’re stagnant. She is decades behind younger voters who are 30 years old and younger in terms of what they believe and what they want.”

The dream scenario here for Chakrabarti is that he taps into voter frustration with Democratic inaction and weakness, and that voters rally around a call to make the 85-year-old Pelosi a symbol of the party’s irrelevance. It would be an echo of Chakrabarti’s old boss, Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, upsetting Rep. Joe Crowley, then the No. 4 Democrat in the House and an heir apparent to the speaker in 2018. He said he sees himself as a part of a wave of Democrats like Mamdani who are challenging establishment incumbents across the country.
